---
title: "Gribouille"
subtitle: "Create elegant graphics with the Grammar of Graphics for Typst."
toc: false
sidebar: false
engine: markdown
include-in-header:
  - text: |
      <style>
        header#title-block-header {
          display: none;
        }
      </style>
---

::: {.hero}

::: {.hero-content}

## ![Gribouille](assets/images/logo.svg){.light-content .img-fluid} ![Gribouille](assets/images/logo-dark.svg){.dark-content .img-fluid}

### Create elegant graphics with the Grammar of Graphics for [Typst]{.ink}.

Compose charts by layering data, aesthetic mappings, geoms, scales, and themes.
The same grammar as [`ggplot2`](https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org) and [`plotnine`](https://plotnine.org), drawn natively in Typst.

[Get started](get-started/index.qmd){.btn .btn-primary .btn-lg}
[View on GitHub](https://github.com/mcanouil/gribouille){.btn .btn-outline .btn-lg}
:::

::: {.showcase-output}

```{typst}
//| output-filename: "/assets/typst-render/index/penguins.svg"
//| alt: "Scatter plot of flipper length versus body mass for Adelie, Chinstrap, and Gentoo penguins, with convex hulls, mean and standard deviation error bars, and annotated species cards describing each group."
#set page(foreground: context {
  let bg = page.fill
  let dark-bg = if bg == auto or bg == none {
    false
  } else if type(bg) == color {
    luma(bg).components().at(0) < 50%
  } else {
    false
  }
  place(
    top + right,
    dx: -0.25cm,
    dy: 0.25cm,
    image(
      if dark-bg {
        "/docs/assets/images/logo-stacked-dark.svg"
      } else {
        "/docs/assets/images/logo-stacked.svg"
      },
      height: 1cm,
    ),
  )
})

#let species-colours = (
  Adelie: rgb("#ff8c00"),
  Chinstrap: rgb("#008B8B"),
  Gentoo: rgb("#800080"),
)

#let species-card(name, note, colour, ink, paper) = context {
  block(
    width: auto,
    inset: 0pt,
    radius: 4pt,
    fill: paper,
    stroke: (paint: colour, thickness: 0.6pt),
  )[
    #set block(spacing: 0pt)

    #block(
      inset: 2pt,
      radius: 4pt,
      fill: colour,
    )[
      #set text(size: 6pt, weight: "bold", fill: if luma(colour)
        .components()
        .at(0)
        < 50% { white } else { black })
      #set par(spacing: 0pt, leading: 0.85em)
      #name
    ]
    #block(inset: (x: 4pt, y: 3pt))[
      #set text(size: 5.4pt, fill: ink)
      #note
    ]
  ]
}

#plot(
  data: penguins,
  mapping: aes(
    x: "flipper-len",
    y: "body-mass",
    colour: "species",
    fill: "species",
    shape: "species",
  ),
  layers: (
    geom-point(size: 2pt, alpha: 0.25, stroke: 0.5pt, colour: rgb("#ffffff")),
    geom-mark(method: "hull", expand: 5pt, alpha: 0.25),
    geom-errorbar(stat: stat-summary(fun: "mean-sd"), width: 5pt),
    geom-errorbarh(stat: stat-summary(fun: "mean-sd"), height: 5pt),
    geom-typst(
      data: (
        (
          x: 183,
          y: 5000,
          species: "Adelie",
          description: "Smallest of the three; white eye-ring.",
        ),
        (
          x: 208,
          y: 2900,
          species: "Chinstrap",
          description: "Thin black band under the chin.",
        ),
        (
          x: 203,
          y: 6150,
          species: "Gentoo",
          description: "Largest brush-tailed; bright orange bill.",
        ),
      ),
      mapping: aes(
        x: "x",
        y: "y",
        label: after-stat((row, ctx) => species-card(
          row.species,
          row.description,
          species-colours.at(row.species),
          ctx.theme.at("ink", default: black),
          ctx.theme.at("paper", default: white),
        )),
      ),
    ),
  ),
  scales: (
    scale-x-continuous(),
    scale-y-continuous(labels: format-comma()),
    scale-colour-discrete(
      limits: species-colours.keys(),
      palette: species-colours.values(),
    ),
    scale-fill-discrete(
      limits: species-colours.keys(),
      palette: species-colours.values(),
    ),
  ),
  labs: labs(
    title: typst("Penguins *Dataset*"),
    subtitle: typst({
      [Flipper length vs body mass by species: ]
      species-colours
        .pairs()
        .map(p => text(fill: p.at(1), weight: "bold")[#p.at(0)])
        .join(", ")
    }),
    caption: "Data from Palmer Archipelago (Antarctica) penguin dataset.",
    colour: "Species",
    fill: "Species",
    shape: "Species",
    x: "Flipper Length (mm)",
    y: "Body Mass (g)",
  ),
  theme: theme-minimal(
    axis-line: element-line(stroke: 0.5pt),
    tick-length: 0.05cm,
  ),
  guides: guides(
    colour: guide-none(),
    fill: guide-none(),
    shape: guide-none(),
  ),
  width: 12cm,
  height: 9cm,
)
```

:::

:::

::: {.steps}
::: {.step}

### 1. Data. {.no-anchor}

Bring a CSV, a list of dictionaries, or an inline table.
:::

::: {.step}

### 2. Mapping. {.no-anchor}

Declare which column drives `x`, `y`, `colour`, `fill`, or `size`.
:::

::: {.step}

### 3. Layers. {.no-anchor}

Stack geoms, scales, facets, and a theme until the plot is right.
:::
:::

## See it in action

::: {.showcase}

::: {.showcase-code}

```typst
#import "@preview/gribouille:{{< env VERSION >}}": *

#plot(
  data: penguins,
  mapping: aes(
    x: "flipper-len",
    y: "body-mass",
    colour: "species",
    fill: "species",
  ),
  layers: (
    geom-point(size: 2pt, alpha: 0.85),
    geom-smooth(method: "lm", alpha: 0.2),
  ),
  facet: facet-wrap("island", labeller: label-both()),
  scales: (scale-y-continuous(labels: format-comma()),),
  labs: labs(
    title: "Penguin Morphology by Island",
    subtitle: "Flipper length versus body mass with a per-species linear fit",
    x: "Flipper Length (mm)",
    y: "Body Mass (g)",
    colour: "Species",
    fill: "Species",
  ),
  theme: theme-minimal(),
  width: 12cm,
  height: 9cm,
)
```

:::

::: {.showcase-output}

```{typst}
//| output-filename: "/assets/typst-render/index/showcase.svg"
//| alt: "Faceted scatter plot of flipper length versus body mass for Adelie, Chinstrap, and Gentoo penguins, split by island, with a linear smoother fitted per species."
#include "/examples/showcase.typ"
```

:::

:::

## What is in the box

::: {.feature-grid}

::: {.feature}

#### [Geoms.](reference/geoms/index.qmd) {.no-anchor}

Points, lines, bars, histograms, smoothers, ribbons, boxplots, text, labels, and reference lines.
:::

::: {.feature}

#### [Scales.](reference/scales/index.qmd) {.no-anchor}

Continuous and discrete x, y, colour, fill, size, shape, linetype, with Viridis and manual palettes.
:::

::: {.feature}

#### [Positions and stats.](reference/index.qmd) {.no-anchor}

Identity, stack, dodge, and fill positions.
Bin, count, identity, and smooth stats.
:::

::: {.feature}

#### [Facets.](reference/facets/index.qmd) {.no-anchor}

`facet-wrap` and `facet-grid` with shared or free scales.
:::

::: {.feature}

#### [Themes.](reference/themes/index.qmd) {.no-anchor}

`theme-minimal`, `theme-classic`, `theme-void`, plus `theme()` element overrides.
:::

::: {.feature}

#### [Composable.](examples/index.qmd) {.no-anchor}

Everything is data you can `let`, extend, and reuse across a Typst document.
:::

:::

## A few examples

::: {.light-content}

::: {.gallery-preview}
[![Multilayer trend](/assets/typst-render/examples/multilayer-light.svg)](examples/index.qmd)
[![Filled contour bands](/assets/typst-render/examples/contour-filled-light.svg)](examples/index.qmd)
[![Area chart](/assets/typst-render/examples/geom-area-light.svg)](examples/index.qmd)
[![Heatmap](/assets/typst-render/examples/geom-tile-light.svg)](examples/index.qmd)
:::

:::

::: {.dark-content}

::: {.gallery-preview}
[![Multilayer trend](/assets/typst-render/examples/multilayer-dark.svg)](examples/index.qmd)
[![Filled contour bands](/assets/typst-render/examples/contour-filled-dark.svg)](examples/index.qmd)
[![Area chart](/assets/typst-render/examples/geom-area-dark.svg)](examples/index.qmd)
[![Heatmap](/assets/typst-render/examples/geom-tile-dark.svg)](examples/index.qmd)
:::

:::

[See all examples](examples/index.qmd){.btn .btn-outline}

::: {.install-band}

## Install.

```typst
#import "@preview/gribouille:{{< env VERSION >}}": *
```

[Read the getting-started guide](get-started/index.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

::: {.fine-print}

_Gribouille_ requires [Typst](https://typst.app) {{< var typst.series >}} or later and [CeTZ](https://typst.app/universe/package/cetz) {{< var dependencies.cetz.series >}} as the drawing backend.
Released under the [MIT Licence](https://github.com/mcanouil/gribouille?tab=MIT-1-ov-file#readme).

:::
